steps.title: Identify what Text 1's claim depends on
steps.content: Text 1 concludes that humans occupied the cave 33,150 years ago based on interpreting crude stone pieces as tools. This entire argument rests on one critical assumption: that these objects were intentionally made by humans, not naturally formed.

steps.title: Match Text 2's objection
steps.content:
Text 2 directly targets the assumption underlying Text 1's claim: that the stone pieces are human-made tools.

The evidence Text 2 provides—they could be naturally fractured, and no other human activity or DNA is present—demonstrates the claim rests on an unsupported assumption, which is exactly what B identifies.

The other choices misread Text 2's skepticism as acceptance of plausibility (A, C) or misattribute claims Text 2 doesn't make (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
